data.title: Gold's daily chart is showing signs of strength, but analysts warn of short-term moving average resistance.
data.description: In a report, RHB Research analyst Joseph Chai noted that the bullish momentum in New York Mercantile Exchange gold futures is accelerating, as seen on the daily chart. The analyst observed that gold prices broke through the $4,500 per ounce resistance level in the most recent trading day. Chai stated that bulls are now targeting the next resistance level. However, he added that gold prices may encounter strong selling pressure near the downtrend 20-day simple moving average (SMA). Data from the London Stock Exchange Group shows that the 20-day moving average is currently around $4,815.63 per ounce. (Jinshi)

data.title: MGBX will launch Based spot trading.
data.description: According to official news, MGBX will launch Based (BASED) spot trading on March 31, 2026 at 18:00 (SGT). Deposits will open at 16:00 (SGT) on March 31, 2026, and trading will open at 18:00 (SGT) on March 31, 2026. Withdrawals will open at 19:00 (SGT) on April 2, 2026.

data.title: Foreign Investors Record Historic Net Sell-off in South Korean Stocks in March
data.description: Foreign investors sold a record 35.9 trillion Korean won worth of South Korean stocks in March, marking the largest monthly net sell-off in history. According to Jin10, this unprecedented divestment reflects growing concerns over market volatility and geopolitical tensions affecting investor sentiment. Analysts suggest that the sell-off could have significant implications for South Korea's financial markets, potentially influencing stock prices and capital flows in the coming months.

data.title: Whale Resumes Long Position on CL with Significant Holdings
data.description: A major investor has reopened a long position on CL, holding 109,646 shares valued at $11.25 million, according to PANews. The leverage used for this position is one-fold. Previously, this investor executed two long trades on CL, earning a profit of $1.75 million.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Analyst Predicts Strengthening Bullish Momentum for Gold Futures
data.description: Gold futures on the New York Commodity Exchange are experiencing a surge in bullish momentum, according to Jin10. RHB Research analyst Joseph Chai noted in his report that the price of gold recently broke through the resistance level of $4,500 per ounce. Chai highlighted that the bulls are now targeting the next resistance level. However, he cautioned that gold prices might face strong selling pressure near the 20-day simple moving average (SMA), which is currently in a downtrend. Data from the London Stock Exchange Group indicates that the 20-day SMA is approximately $4,815.63 per ounce.

data.title: A whale that had previously profited $1.75 million from a long position in CL has reopened a long position in CL at $11.25 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale has reopened a long position of 109,600 CL tokens, worth $11.25 million, with a leverage ratio of 1x. This whale had previously gone long on CL twice, accumulating a profit of $1.75 million.

data.title: OKX Planet has been upgraded, introducing intelligent algorithm recommendations and integrating trading components.
data.description: According to official news, OKX has completed the upgrade of its community product, OKX Planet (Orbit). The new version features a redesigned content page, introducing personalized algorithm recommendations and embedding components for spot, contract, and strategy trading within posts, allowing users to directly complete trading operations while browsing content. Simultaneously, the information page has also been upgraded, adding modules such as a long-form article subscription channel, the OKX AI trading assistant, and a VIP exclusive service group, aiming to optimize the overall user experience. OKX Planet was launched in January 2026, and this upgrade represents its first major iteration since launch, focusing on improving the efficiency of connecting content and trading, and further enhancing the KOL and creator ecosystem.

data.title: Japanese and South Korean stocks closed lower.
data.description: The Nikkei 225 index closed down 822.13 points, or 1.58%, at 51,063.72 on Tuesday, March 31. The South Korean KOSPI index closed down 224.7 points, or 4.26%, at 5,052.6 on Tuesday, March 31. (Jinshi)

data.title: Goldman Sachs Maintains Bullish Outlook on Gold Despite Recent Sell-Off
data.description:
Goldman Sachs has maintained its optimistic stance on gold, predicting a resurgence in prices by the end of 2026, according to Jin10. Analysts Lina Thomas and Daan Struyven highlighted that the medium-term outlook for gold remains robust. They attribute this to continued gold purchases by central banks and the expectation of two more interest rate cuts in the U.S. this year, which could push gold prices to $5,400 per ounce.

However, the analysts warned of short-term 'tactical downside risks' for gold prices, particularly if energy supply shocks worsen, potentially driving prices down to $3,800 per ounce. Despite these risks, they noted that geopolitical tensions, such as a potential war involving Iran, could lead countries to diversify away from 'traditional Western assets,' thereby increasing gold's upward potential.

The report also addressed concerns about some central banks possibly selling gold to support their currencies, suggesting that such fears are unlikely to materialize. Instead, Gulf countries are more inclined to intervene by reducing their holdings of U.S. Treasury bonds. Assuming no additional private sector investment, the analysts expect medium-term price volatility to moderate, allowing the pace of official sector gold purchases to accelerate again, with an average monthly purchase of about 60 tons.

data.title: Polls show Trump's approval rating has dropped to 33%.
data.description: According to The Hill, a poll released Monday by the University of Massachusetts Amherst showed that Trump's approval rating has fallen to a new low, with fewer respondents expressing support for his administration's war against Iran. 33% of respondents approved of Trump's job performance. Of the 62% who disapproved, 53% expressed "strong" disapproval. (Jinshi)

data.title: U.S. President Trump's Approval Rating Hits New Low in Recent Poll
data.description: A recent poll conducted by the University of Massachusetts Amherst reveals a significant decline in U.S. President Donald Trump's approval ratings. According to Odaily, the survey indicates that only 33% of respondents approve of Trump's performance, marking a new low. Furthermore, the poll highlights that fewer participants support his administration's actions regarding the conflict with Iran. Among the 62% who disapprove of his job performance, 53% express strong disapproval.

data.title: Goldman Sachs: Maintains bullish outlook for gold; the underlying logic for the upward trend remains unchanged.
data.description: Despite the recent gold price sell-off, Goldman Sachs maintains its bullish view on gold, predicting a renewed upward trend by the end of 2026. Analysts Lina Thomas and Daan Struyven stated in a report that the medium-term outlook for gold remains solid. Gold prices are expected to reach $5,400 per ounce due to continued central bank gold purchases and two more expected US interest rate cuts this year. They noted that gold prices still face "tactical downside risks" in the short term, potentially falling to $3,800 per ounce if energy supply shocks worsen further. Nevertheless, if the Iran war prompts countries to accelerate the reduction of "traditional Western assets" and diversify their portfolios, the upside potential for gold remains significant. The report also mentioned that concerns about some central banks potentially selling gold to support their currencies are unlikely to materialize. Gulf states are more inclined to intervene by reducing their holdings of US Treasury bonds. Assuming no additional private sector investment, analysts expect medium-term price volatility to moderate, which will lead to a renewed acceleration in official gold purchases, averaging approximately 60 tons per month. (Jinshi)

data.title: Binance Wallet will launch a prediction market; integrating predict.fun as a provider.
data.description: According to official sources, Binance Wallet will launch a prediction market, integrating predict.fun as a provider.

data.title: Dubai Introduces Regulatory Framework for Crypto Derivatives Trading
data.description:
Dubai's Virtual Assets Regulatory Authority has unveiled a formal regulatory framework for cryptocurrency exchange-traded derivatives. According to Foresight News, the new regulations permit retail customers to engage in trading following a suitability assessment. The framework addresses requirements such as customer suitability, leverage and margin controls, asset segregation, disclosure standards, and regulatory intervention powers. Retail investors are subject to a leverage cap of five times, and exchanges must restrict access to products deemed unsuitable for specific customer groups.

VARA stated that during periods of market stress or trading disorder, regulators have the authority to intervene, including suspending products, mandating position closures, and increasing margin requirements. In emergencies, immediate action may be required without prior notice.

data.title: UK House Prices Unexpectedly Surge Amid Early Iran Conflict
data.description: UK house prices saw an unexpected rise in March, according to data from one of Britain's largest mortgage lenders. Despite the onset of the Iran conflict, demand appeared resilient, with the average house value increasing by 0.9% to £277,186, surpassing the previous month's 0.3% rise. This marks the largest increase since December 2024, defying economists' expectations of stability. According to Jin10, the report provides an early snapshot of UK housing demand following the February 28th Israeli attack on Iran. The conflict has raised borrowing costs and caused global market turbulence, yet buyers showed strong resilience initially. On an annual basis, house prices rose by 2.2%. Private sector data indicates that despite weak official figures in January, particularly in London, the UK real estate market started the year positively. The Bank of England reported that housing loans grew in February for the first time in five months.

data.title: Germany's February Retail Sales Decline by 0.6%, Missing Expectations
data.description: Germany's retail sales in February fell by 0.6%, contrary to the expected growth of 0.2%, according to Jin10. The previous month's retail sales had also decreased by 0.9%, indicating a continued downward trend in consumer spending. This unexpected decline raises concerns about the strength of Germany's consumer market and its potential impact on the broader economy.

data.title: Germany's February Import Price Index Rises by 0.3%, Below Expectations
data.description: Germany's import price index for February increased by 0.3%, falling short of the anticipated 0.8%, according to Jin10. The previous month's figure was recorded at 1.10%. This data reflects a slower growth in import prices than expected, which could have implications for the country's economic outlook and inflationary pressures.

data.title: UK GDP Growth Remains Steady in Q4
data.description: The United Kingdom's GDP growth for the fourth quarter has been confirmed at 0.1%, meeting expectations, according to Jin10. This figure remains unchanged from the previous quarter's growth rate of 0.10%. The steady growth indicates a stable economic environment as the country navigates ongoing global economic challenges.

data.title: Singapore Faces Rising Electricity Prices Amid Global Energy Supply Strain
data.description: Singapore is expected to experience an increase in electricity prices in the coming months due to the ongoing US-Israeli conflict with Iran, which is impacting global energy supplies. Bloomberg posted on X that authorities have issued warnings about the potential for higher costs as the geopolitical tensions continue to affect the availability of energy resources worldwide. The situation underscores the interconnected nature of global energy markets and the potential for regional conflicts to have far-reaching economic consequences.

data.title: Saudi Arabia's Foreign Direct Investment Reaches 48.4 Billion Riyals in Q4
data.description: According to Jin10, official data reveals that Saudi Arabia's net inflow of foreign direct investment reached 48.4 billion riyals in the fourth quarter of last year. During the same period, the unemployment rate in Saudi Arabia stood at 7.2%.
